title: OpenAI’s existential questions
abstract: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss OpenAI's latest acquisitions and whether they address "two big existential problems" for the company.

title: The 12-month window
abstract: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn put a customer satellite in the wrong orbit during its third launch
abstract: The first major failure of Blue Origin's new heavy-launch system could create delays to its ambition to help NASA and the Trump administration return to the Moon.

title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber enters its assetmaxxing era
abstract: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

title: Cracks are starting to form on fusion energy’s funding boom
abstract: Fusion startups and investors risk deepening fissures if some key disagreements aren't settled amicably.

title: VC Ron Conway says he has a ‘rare form of cancer’
abstract: Conway wrote that he “will be stepping back from some of my usual activities,” but he will “continue to support” founders backed by his firm SV Angel.

title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
abstract: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
abstract: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
abstract: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

title: Once close enough for an acquisition, Stripe and Airwallex are now going after each other
abstract: For most of its life, Airwallex and Stripe have mostly operated in different geographies, selling to different buyers. That's changing.

title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
abstract: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
abstract: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.

title: Sources: Cursor in talks to raise $2B+ at $50B valuation as enterprise growth surges
abstract: Returning backers a16z and Thrive are expected to lead the round.

title: Hackers are abusing unpatched Windows security flaws to hack into organizations
abstract: A security researcher published details of three security vulnerabilities in Windows Defender, and the code used to exploit them. Now, hackers are taking advantage of the vulnerabilities in real-life attacks, according to a cybersecurity firm.

title: Harshita Arora Joins YC as General Partner
abstract: We're thrilled to announce that Harshita Arora is joining Y Combinator as a General Partner. She brings deep fintech and infrastructure experience, a founder's instinct for product, and the perspective of someone who's been building companies since she was a teenager.

title: Adding Canada Back to Our List of Accepted Countries of Incorporation
abstract: After hearing feedback from Canadian founders in our network, we are adding Canada back to our list of accepted countries of incorporation.

title: Congratulations to EquipmentShare on Going Public
abstract: Today, EquipmentShare (YC W15) goes public. From Missouri to 373 locations nationwide, they've built the operating system construction has been missing—built by contractors, for contractors. Congrats to Jabbok, Willy, and the EquipmentShare team on a huge milestone.

title: Announcing the YC AI Stack
abstract: We've partnered with nearly two dozen companies to offer students who attend YC events a suite of over $25,000 in free credits for AI devtools.

title: 2026 Demo Day Dates
abstract: We’re excited to share the Demo Day dates for 2026 so founders, investors, and our broader community can plan ahead.

title: Meesho Goes Public
abstract: Meesho goes public today. From S16 to IPO, they’ve built one of India’s largest e-commerce platforms—empowering millions of small merchants to grow their businesses. Congrats to Vidit and Sanjeev on a decade of relentless building and listening to users!

title: Congratulations to Groww
abstract: Today, Groww, India's leading digital investing platform, goes public. Tens of millions use Groww to invest simply and confidently in stocks, mutual funds, and ETFs. As YC's first company to list in India, we couldn't be more proud.

title: BillionToOne Goes Public — The Startup That Made Genetic Testing Universal
abstract: Today, BillionToOne goes public, becoming YC's fourth publicly traded biotech company. More importantly, they have built one of the most important genetic tests in the U.S. Today, 1 in 11 babies born in the US are tested with BillionToOne's fetal genetic test.

title: Meet YC's Newest Visiting Partners
abstract: We're excited to announce that nine exceptional founders are joining us as Visiting Partners at YC: Matt Riley, Harshita Arora, Grey Baker, Christopher Golda, Raphael Schaad, Christina Gilbert, Francois Chaubard, Vivian Shen, and James Evans.

title: YC x Coinbase RFS: Build Onchain
abstract: We are entering the era of Fintech 3.0. Regulatory clarity, growing consumer adoption, and low-cost chains have paved the way for a golden age of building in crypto — and at YC, Base, and Coinbase, we want to fund builders to seize this moment.

title: Ankit Gupta Joins YC as General Partner, Bringing Deep ML Expertise
abstract: We’re thrilled to announce that Ankit Gupta is joining YC as our newest General Partner. Ankit has already worked with dozens of YC founders as a visiting partner in recent batches. He has a rare blend of deep machine learning expertise and firsthand startup experience that make him

title: Dalton Caldwell’s Move to Partner Emeritus
abstract: After 12+ years, 25 batches, and working with more than a thousand startups, Dalton Caldwell is transitioning to Partner Emeritus at YC. Dalton joined YC in 2011 and has contributed in a number of roles over the years, most recently as a Managing Partner. During his time here, he worked

title: Welcoming Jon Xu and Andrew Miklas as YC’s Newest General Partners
abstract: Over the last few batches, visiting partners Jon Xu and Andrew Miklas have become two of the most trusted voices in the YC community. They were both founders in YC’s Summer 2010 batch, and now, 15 years and two industry-shaping companies later, they’re back in a new role: General Partners at YC.

title: Tyler Bosmeny built Clever into a $500M company–now he’s helping YC founders do the same as General Partner
abstract: We’re thrilled to welcome Tyler Bosmeny back to YC, this time as a General Partner.

title: Michael Seibel's Legacy Continues at YC: Transition to Partner Emeritus
abstract: If you’ve been around YC, you know Michael Seibel has been a huge part of it for over a decade. He’s done it all - YC Founder (w07, w12), Group Partner, and Managing Director/CEO of the YC accelerator. Along the way, he’s helped

title: Truecaller faces mounting pressures as its growth matures
abstract: As growth slows, Truecaller is leaning on subscriptions, business services, and new features to sustain momentum beyond India.

title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
abstract: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
abstract: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
abstract: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

title: The climate tech IPO window could finally be cracking open
abstract: Nuclear startup X-energy went public, geothermal startup Fervo is about to. Could this be the moment that climate tech investors have been waiting for?

title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
abstract: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
abstract: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

title: Lachy Groom to back India startup Pronto at a $200M valuation, sources say
abstract: This round, should it occur, would double the house-help startup's valuation in a matter of weeks.

title: Steve Ballmer blasts founder he backed who pleaded guilty to fraud: ‘I was duped and feel silly’
abstract: Steve Ballmer wrote a fiery letter in the sentencing of disgraced founder Joseph Sanberg documenting all the harm that's befalling him as an investor.

title: Cohere acquires, merges with Germany-based startup to create a ‘transatlantic AI powerhouse’
abstract: Cohere, the Canada-based AI company that makes AI tools for businesses in regulated industries, announced Friday it would merge with Aleph Alpha, a German company that also builds AI systems for businesses and governments.

title: India’s Snabbit closes $56M round as investor interest in on-demand home services heats up
abstract: Snabbit now processes over 40,000 daily jobs and has cut costs sharply as it expands across cities and services.

title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
abstract: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
abstract: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
abstract: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
abstract: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
abstract: There have been plenty of rumors about OpenAI's hardware plans, which involve launching a pair of earbuds. A new note from industry analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggests that the AI company might be working on a phone in collaboration with MediaTek, Qualcomm, and Luxshare.

title: How one venture firm is investing in an increasingly fragmented world
abstract: Geopolitical turmoil has made venture investing challenging, leading Kompas VC to carve out a niche in startups focused on the physical world.

title: At his OpenAI trial, Musk relitigates an old friendship
abstract: It's a story Musk has told before -- in interviews and to author Walter Isaacson for his bestselling biography of Musk -- but Tuesday was the first time he said it under oath.

title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
abstract: A day after OpenAI got Microsoft to agree to end exclusive rights, AWS announced a slate of OpenAI model offerings, including a new agent service.

title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
abstract: Amazon's new "Join the chat" feature lets you ask questions about products and receive AI-powered audio responses.

title: Match Group invests $100M in Sniffies, a cruising app for gay men
abstract: The app is Match Group's newest attempt to get mobile users excited about online romance again.

title: Google expands Pentagon’s access to its AI after Anthropic’s refusal
abstract: After Anthropic refused to allow the DoD to use its AI for domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons, Google has signed a new contract with the department.

title: Founder of Shark Tank-backed startup Scholly sues his acquirer Sallie Mae
abstract: Chris Gray is suing his startup’s acquirer, Sallie Mae, for wrongful termination and alleging it's selling student data through a subsidiary. Sallie Mae denies the allegations and vows to fight.

title: Snapchat brings AI-powered conversational advertising to its app
abstract: Users will be able to chat with a brand's AI agent to do things like ask questions and get recommendations.

title: YouTube is testing an AI-powered search feature that shows guided answers
abstract: YouTube is rolling out the new AI search feature to Premium subscribers in the U.S. on an opt-in basis.

title: BCI startup Neurable looks to license its ‘mind-reading’ tech for consumer wearables
abstract: The startup specializes in "non-invasive" "mind-reading" tech — a kind of neural data collection that, its CEO hopes, will have all sorts of consumer applications.

title: Red Hat’s OpenClaw maintainer just made enterprise Claw deployments a lot safer
abstract: Tank OS puts OpenClaw AI agents into a container that lets it run reliably and more safely, especially for those running fleets of them.

title: Otter’s new feature lets users search across their enterprise tools
abstract: With this launch, users can connect their Gmail, Google Drive, Notion, Jira, and Salesforce accounts and query that data along with existing meeting data. The company said that it will soon allow connections with Microsoft Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, and Slack.

title: Drizzle on top: A new high-end dog food brand is coming for the 1%
abstract: Golden Child is launching with two "five-star" products: a fresh frozen meal system and, more intriguingly, a "drizzle." It has also raised $37 million in funding.

title: SoftBank is creating a robotics company that builds data centers — and already eyeing a $100B IPO
abstract: You need infrastructure to build AI a and robots, but apparently you also need AI and robots to build infrastructure.

title: Amazon’s cloud business is surging — and so is its capital spending
abstract: The e-commerce giant is making more money than expected from AWS but it's also spending a lot, and will continue to do so in the near term, its chief executive said.

title: Sources: Anthropic could raise a new $50B round at a valuation of $900B
abstract: The maker of Claude has received multiple pre-emptive offers at valuations in the $850 billion to $900 billion range, according to sources familiar with the matter.

title: On the stand, Elon Musk can’t escape his own tweets
abstract: Elon Musk took the stand for the second day for his attempt to legally dismantle OpenAI.

title: Meta is still burning money on AR/VR
abstract: Meta is losing billions on Reality Labs each quarter, and its AI expenditures are only going to increase its spending.

title: Satya Nadella says he’s ready to ‘exploit’ the new OpenAI deal
abstract: Microsoft gets to offer OpenAI's tech to its cloud customers and doesn't have to pay for it. "We fully plan to exploit it," Nadella said.

title: Microsoft says it has over 20M paid Copilot users, and they really are using it
abstract: Despite the lingering perception that no one really uses Copilot, Microsoft said on Wednesday that the number of users and engagement is growing.

title: Google Cloud surpasses $20B, but says growth was capacity-constrained
abstract: Google Cloud topped $20B in quarterly revenue for the first time, fueled by surging demand for AI. But capacity constraints mean it could have grown even faster.

title: Google gains 25M subscriptions in Q1, driven by YouTube and Google One
abstract: Google added 25M paid subscriptions in Q1, reaching 350M total, as YouTube and Google One grow.

title: Fusion power startup Zap Energy pulls a partial pivot, adding nuclear fission to the mix
abstract: Surprise! Fusion startup Zap Energy says it will be developing fission reactors alongside its fusion devices.

title: Parallel Web Systems hits $2B valuation five months after its last big raise
abstract: The AI agent-tool startup founded by former Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal has raised $100 million, led by Sequoia, months after raising a previous $100 million.

title: Google Photos uses AI to make the iconic closet from ‘Clueless’ a reality
abstract: Google says the new feature will leverage AI technology to automatically create a copy of your wardrobe that's based on the pieces of clothing appearing in your Google Photos library.

title: Bill Gurley, Jack Altman back startup Pursuit, which helps companies sell to government
abstract: On Wednesday, Pursuit announced a $22 million Series A round led by Mike Rosengarten, the co-founder of OpenGov, with big-name VCs participating.

title: Uber is in the hotel business now, thanks in part to AI
abstract: Uber announced several new features on Wednesday during its annual event, which push far beyond the company's original ride-hailing purpose and deeper into its users' lives.

title: Earth AI is vertically integrating the search for critical minerals
abstract: When Earth AI started hitting months-long delays in its search for critical minerals, it decided to take matters into its own hands.

title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a hit in India, but not a big winner elsewhere, yet
abstract: Users in India are embracing ChatGPT Images 2.0 for creative, personal visuals — from avatars to cinematic portraits.

title: As Tim Cook steps down, Apple hit record sales — but a chip shortage looms
abstract: Cook warned that Apple is facing supply-chain headwinds from RAMageddon that could impact its business.

title: Y Combinator alum Skio sells for $105M cash, only raised $8M, founder says
abstract: Subscription billing fintech Skio sold to its competitor Recharge in what was a healthy exit, according to its founder and former CEO.

title: Sources: Anthropic potential $900B+ valuation round could happen within 2 weeks
abstract: Anthropic is asking investors to submit allocations for the AI company’s latest fundraise within the next 48 hours, according to sources familiar with the matter.

title: Apple was surprised by AI-driven demand for Macs
abstract: Apple said it will be supply-constrained on Mac mini, Studio, and Neo in the next quarter, too.

title: Legal AI startup Legora hits $5.6B valuation and its battle with Harvey just got hotter
abstract: The two wildly fast-growing rivals have raised massive sums, pushed into each other's home turf, and now have dueling ad campaigns.

title: Hackers are actively exploiting a bug in cPanel, used by millions of websites
abstract: Web hosts are scrambling to fix the bug under active attack by hackers. One company said hackers have been abusing the bug for months.

title: After dissing Anthropic for limiting Mythos, OpenAI restricts access to Cyber, too
abstract: OpenAI will begin rolling out its cybersecurity testing tool, GPT-5.5 Cyber only "to critical cyber defenders" at first.

title: EV startup Faraday Future paid $7.5M to company tied to founder Jia Yueting
abstract: The perpetually struggling EV company made the payments while being investigated by the SEC. That four-year probe was ultimately closed in March.

title: OpenAI announces new advanced security for ChatGPT accounts, including a partnership with Yubico
abstract: OpenAI is launching additional opt-in protections for ChatGPT accounts. The new security initiative includes a new partnership with security key provider Yubico.

title: Elon Musk testifies that xAI trained Grok on OpenAI models
abstract: "Distillation" is a hot topic as frontier labs try to prevent smaller competitors from copying their models.

title: FDA approval, fundraising, and the reality of building in healthcare according to BioticsAI founder
abstract: BioticsAI CEO Robhy Bustami joined Isabelle Johannessen on Build Mode to discuss how the company has navigated a highly regulated space and kept the team motivated while cutting through all the red tape.

title: Google’s Gemini AI assistant is hitting the road in millions of vehicles
abstract: The move signals Google’s push to bring more advanced, conversational AI into the driving experience.

title: Stripe introduces Link, a digital wallet that autonomous AI agents can use, too
abstract: Link lets users connect cards, banks, and subscriptions, then authorize AI agents to spend securely via approval flows.

title: Salesforce is crowdsourcing its AI roadmap — with customers
abstract: Salesforce lets its customers lead its product roadmap with the thinking that if one enterprise customer has a problem, the others likely do too.

title: SpaceX backer 137 Ventures raises $700M for two growth-stage funds
abstract: VC firm 137 Ventures has raised over $700 million to back growth-stage startups. Its portfolio includes SpaceX, Anduril, Hadrian.

title: Dental practice software maker fixes bug that exposed patients’ medical records
abstract: The security bug is now fixed, but the patient who found it said it was challenging to alert the software company about the issue.

title: Musely secures $360M from General Catalyst without giving up equity
abstract: The DTC skin, hair, and menopause care brand will use the non-dilutive capital to super-charge customer acquisition.

title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
abstract: Meta bought humanoid startup Assured Robot Intelligence to beef up its AI models for robots, the company said.

title: Coatue has a plan to buy up land for data centers, possibly for Anthropic
abstract: Coatue, one of the biggest names in venture capital, has a new venture that is reportedly buying land near large power sources.

title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
abstract: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.

title: Ubuntu services hit by outages after DDoS attack
abstract: A group of hacktivists have claimed responsibility for a distributed denial-of-service attack, which has affected several Ubuntu and Canonical websites, and prevented users from updating the Linux-based operating system.

title: Musk v. Altman is just getting started
abstract: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets are surfacing in court, and there are plenty more witnesses to come. Musk’s argument against OpenAI? By converting the company to a for-profit model, Sam Altman betrayed the “nonprofit for the […]

title: People are finally using Reddit’s search
abstract: The company saw a 30% year-on-year jump in the number of people using search every week, CEO Steve Huffman said on Thursday.
